description: 86-line conference planning conversation with 4 speakers coordinating travel and schedule for a 3-day developer conference. Tests extracting team events from logistical planning, distinguishing shared calendar events from personal session bookings.

events.title: DevConf Networking Reception
events.reasoning: Bob mentions the opening networking reception at 7 PM Sunday at the conference hotel rooftop. Alice plans for the whole team to arrive by 6 PM and attend together. All four confirm they are going.

events.title: Speaker Reception
events.reasoning: Dave mentions a speaker reception Monday at 6 PM in the ballroom. He can bring the others as guests and Alice confirms everyone should attend.
events.assumptions: Duration approximately 1 hour based on context

events.title: Panel Prep Dinner
events.reasoning: Alice and Dave agree to a Monday evening dinner at 7:30 PM (updated from 7 PM to accommodate the speaker reception) for Dave to walk through his panel talking points. All four will attend.

events.title: Distributed Caching Panel
events.reasoning: Dave is on the distributed caching panel at 11 AM Tuesday March 4th for approximately 1 hour. Alice and Carol both confirm they will attend to support him.

events.title: Conference Debrief
events.reasoning: Alice schedules a post-conference debrief for Thursday March 6th at 2 PM for 1 hour in the team room for all four team members to share learnings and define action items.

mock_llm_response.summary: Found 5 team calendar events: Sunday networking reception (Mar 2 7 PM), Monday speaker reception (Mar 3 6 PM) and panel prep dinner (Mar 3 7:30 PM), Tuesday Dave's panel (Mar 4 11 AM), and Thursday conference debrief (Mar 6 2 PM). Individual conference sessions (Bob's Kubernetes workshop, Carol's Figma workshop) are personal and not included as shared events.

notes: Reference datetime is Friday 2026-02-20. Conference is March 3-5 in Chicago with travel on March 2. Individual sessions like Bob's Kubernetes workshop (Mon 1-4 PM) and Carol's Figma workshop (Tue 2-5 PM) are explicitly called out as personal calendar items, not team events. The pre-conference dinner reservation is a personal booking handled outside the calendar.
